title: SSVEP paradigm of the Mobile BCI dataset
description: This dataset comprises steady-state visually evoked potential (SSVEP) recordings from 23 healthy participants performing a brain-computer interface (BCI) task during various locomotion states (standing, walking, running). The study employed 73-channel EEG acquisition at 100 Hz with three visual flicker stimuli at 5.45, 8.57, and 12.0 Hz, collected across four sessions per subject. This derivative dataset is processed using the Mother of All BCI Benchmarks (MOABB) framework and serves as a benchmark for evaluating mobile BCI performance in naturalistic movement conditions.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 73-channel BrainAmp system (Brain Product GmbH) at 100 Hz sampling rate with Ag/AgCl electrodes referenced to FCz and grounded at Fpz. Participants viewed visual flicker stimuli at three frequencies (5.45, 8.57, 12.0 Hz) during 5-second trials across multiple locomotion conditions. Four EOG channels monitored eye movements. Signal processing included power spectral analysis across frequency bands (delta, theta, alpha, beta) and classification using regularized Linear Discriminant Analysis (rLDA) and Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA).
